title: dryopteris subtetragona
intro: Dryopteris subtetragona, also known as the two-tiered wood fern, is a species of fern that belongs to the Dryopteris genus and is native to East Asia. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Dryopteris subtetragona include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and support immune function, making it a popular remedy for colds and flu. Dryopteris subtetragona can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, allowing for its medicinal properties to be harnessed and utilized for therapeutic purposes.
